question_text: A sealed container holds a gas at room temperature. When the container is heated, the pressure inside increases. Which explanation uses the particle model to explain this?

options.label: A
options.text: Heating makes the container larger, so there is more space for particles

options.label: B
options.text: Heating makes particles move faster, so they collide with the walls more frequently and forcefully, increasing pressure

options.label: C
options.text: Heating creates new particles inside the container

options.label: D
options.text: Heating causes the particles to expand and take up more individual space

explanation: When temperature increases, particles gain kinetic energy and move faster. Faster particles hit the container walls with greater frequency and force, which increases pressure. This is a direct application of the particle model linking temperature to particle motion.
